sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Intersect governance coordination, technical stewardship, incident response, release coordination, repository management, security initiatives, and management of critical processes for June 2026 to June 2027.
Recipient: Intersect via the 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stated in the on-chain rewards.

Deliverables: WP 1 - Intersect operations and ecosystem coordination: 6,000,000 ada, WP 2 - Technical Stewardship, Incident Response & Coordination including core Cardano repositories: 18,800,000 ada, WP 3 - Management of critical processes: 600,000 ada, Use of milestone-based drawdowns, evidence-backed reporting, transparent disclosures, and external assurance by Appold, Use of Sundae Labs treasury management smart contract framework with TRSC/PSSC controls and oversight committee checks

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Intersect requests 25,400,000 ada for a June 2026 to June 2027 core operating model covering governance coordination, technical stewardship, incident response, release coordination, and critical unowned processes.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The budget is divided into 6,000,000 ada for operations and ecosystem coordination, 18,800,000 ada for technical stewardship and incident response, and 600,000 ada for management of critical processes.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Intersect says the overall ask has been reduced from $7.875M last year to $6.35M while preserving the most critical functions for Cardano continuity and maturity.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Intersect says it has coordinated two network upgrades, is preparing for a third, and led ecosystem coordination during the November 2025 chain partition incident.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal says it achieved the required 67% support threshold in the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase and was advanced under the approved Budget Process Framework.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal states that the requested withdrawal does not, at submission, breach the 350M Net Change Limit covering Epoch 613 to Epoch 713, either alone or in aggregate.
missing: Independent evidence for: Intersect says the overall ask has been reduced from $7.875M last year to $6.35M while preserving the most critical functions for Cardano continuity and maturity.
Independent evidence for: Intersect says it has coordinated two network upgrades, is preparing for a third, and led ecosystem coordination during the November 2025 chain partition incident.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Intersect requests 25,400,000 ada for a June 2026 to June 2027 core operating model covering governance coordination, technical stewardship, incident response, release coordination, and critical unowned processes." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Intersect says the overall ask has been reduced from $7.875M last year to $6.35M while preserving the most critical functions for Cardano continuity and maturity." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
